sections.paragraph:
m. שְׁלֵמָה f. adj.-

(1) whole, perfect,-(a) i.e. of full and just number and measure, as אֶבֶן שְׁלֵמָה a just weight, Deu 25:15 compare Gen 15:16 (where it is used of a full and just measure of sins); גָּלוּת שְׁלֵמָה a full number of captives, Amo 1:6, .-

(b) sound, safe, Gen 33:18 of an army, Nah 1:12. אֲבָנִים שָׁלֵמֹות are stones which have been untouched, are unviolated by iron, i.e. rough, unhewn Deu 27:6 1Ki 6:7.

(2) completed, finished, 2Ch 8:16.

(3) cherishing peace and friendship (see the root in Pual, Hiphil, Hophal). Gen 34:21, שְׁלֵמִים הֵם אִתָּנוּ “they live peaceably with us;” specially שׁ׳ עִם יְהֹוָה devoted to God, at peace with him, 1Ki 8:61 11:4 15:3 , 14 1Ki 15:14and without these words, 2Ki 20:3 1Ch 28:9 2Ch 15:17. Compare Hiphil No. 2; (also صُسْلِمُ devoted to God and to Mahomet, one who professes the Mahometan religion.)

(4) [Salem, Shalem], pr.n. i.q. יְרוּשָׁלֵם Jerusalem, as to the etymology of which, see p. 367, A , Gen 14:18 Psa 76:3. Josephus ( Antiq. i. 10, § 2 ), τὴν μέντοι Σόλυμα ὕστερον ἐκάλεσαν Ἱεροσόλυμα. See Relandi Palæstina, p. 976. (Arab. ثَلَمُ, ثَلَّمُ id.).
